name: java-runtime
description: Matches the Java a Minecraft version needs against what's installed and downloads the right runtime on mismatch — the Java half, not heap size. Use when the game fails to start on a Java-version error or the user asks about Java/JDK/JRE ("java不对","java版本","装java","UnsupportedClassVersionError"); memory allocation is set-memory.
body:
# Java runtime compatibility

## Version table (memorise)
- MC ≤1.16.x → Java 8 (some tolerate 11)
- 1.17–1.17.1 → Java 16+
- 1.18–1.20.4 → Java 17+
- 1.20.5+ / 1.21+ → Java 21+
Forge on old versions is strict about Java 8; modern NeoForge follows the game's rule.

## Diagnose
1. `instance(action=list_java)` — discovered runtimes (version + path).
2. `instance(action=details, instance)` — game version implies required Java from above.
3. Wrong Java shows up as an immediate crash with `UnsupportedClassVersionError`.

## Fix
`instance(action=download_java, gameVersion or javaVersion)` — likely backgrounded; call
`job(action=check, jobId)` before declaring done. HMCL auto-selects a suitable Java per
instance by default; only pin one manually when auto-selection picked wrong. There is no
`instance` action for pinning — it's a raw JSON edit (`javaType`/`customJavaPath` fields in
`game-settings.json` / `instance-game-settings.json`) via the `config-hmcl` skill.

32-bit Java caps around 1.5 GB heap — if `instance(action=list_java)` shows only 32-bit, download 64-bit.

## Don't
Don't install Java via shell/winget — `instance(action=download_java)` keeps it managed by HMCL.
